revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fr:Ishizuchi-jinja]][[ja:石鎚神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Ishizuchi Jinja Honsha 01.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Saijo|qid=Q633945|1=Saijō, Ehime}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.889806|N|133.1555|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}
{{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Isidutizinzya honsya.jpg }}
{{ill|Ishizuchi Shrine|ja|石鎚神社|6=en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizuchi Shrine|qq=}} (石鎚神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Shikoku]]<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last=|first=|title=Ishizuchi-jinja Shrine {{!}} Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)|url=https://www.japan.travel/en/spot/236/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=Travel Japan|publisher={{ill|Japan National Tourism Organization|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan National Tourism Organization|qq=}}|language=en-us|qq=draft}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Ishizuchi Shrine |url=https://shikokutours.com/attractions/Ehime-Prefecture/points-of-interest/ishizuchi_shrine |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=shikokutours.com |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Japan |first=Visit Ehime |title=Ishizuchi Shrine |url=https://www.visitehimejapan.com/en/see-and-do/127 |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=Visit Ehime Japan |language=en}}</ref> It is located on Mount Ishizuchi.<ref name=":0" /> It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>
Mt. Ishizuchi holds significance, as one of Japans seven mountains. Is a place of reverence for mountain worship. Konjikiin Maegamiji serves as the temple of the Shingon sect of Buddhism.<ref name=":1" />
It is part of the Shikoku 88 Temple Pilgrimage. The shrine worships {{ill|Mountain God|en|Mountain God|qid=Q16256547|lt=Mountain Gods|1=Mountain God}}.<ref name=":0" />
== History == Ishizuchi Shrine and {{ill|Maegami-ji|ja|前神寺|de|lt=Maegami-ji temple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maegami-ji|1=Maegami-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11397792}} have had an intrinsically linked set of histories, both coming from the same original origin.<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1" />
Ishizuchisan Konjikiin Maegamiji, commonly referred to as Temple 64 is situated at the base of Mt. Ishizuchi, which stands tall at 1982 meters. The temples roots can be traced back to the era of Emperor Tenmu (673 686 AD). [[Ozunu]], the founder of Shugendō had an encounter with Sakyamuni and Amida in the form of Zaō Gongen on Mt. Ishizuchi upon completing ascetic practices. In commemoration of this experience he. Enshrined statues representing them. Subsequently during [[Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu's]] reign (781 806 AD) a seven building temple complex was established following his recovery from illness through prayer; it was named Konjikiin/Maegamiji.<ref name=":1" />
Throughout history various emperors such as [[Emperor Buntoku]], {{ill|Emperor Takakura|en|Emperor Takakura|qid=Q357261|lt=Emperor Takakura|1=Emperor Takakura}}, {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|en|Emperor Go-Toba|qid=Q316658|lt=Emperor Gotoba|1=Emperor Go-Toba}} and {{ill|Emperor Juntoku|en|Emperor Juntoku|qid=Q357249|lt=Emperor Juntoku|1=Emperor Juntoku}} showed devotion to the temple.<ref name=":1" />
The renowned figure Kukai or Kōbō Daishi made two visits, to the temple. Partook in ascetic rituals including Goma training and fasting.<ref name=":1" />
During the Edo period and beyond the temple thrived with support, from Lord Saijo of the Matsudaira clan, who even gifted a three leaved temple emblem. It underwent reconstruction in 1882 during the year of the Meiji era. Eventually became the main center of the Shingon sect.<ref name=":1" />
A year on July 1st the temple hosts the "Opening of the Mountains" ceremony. This event brings together laypeople dressed in robes who recite "Nanmaida" while conch shells resonate in harmony.<ref name=":1" />
== Access == To reach the shrine one can embark on a short train journey from Saijo city. Travelers can board the Yosan Line Shiokaze Limited Express train from Matsuyama to Iyo Saijo station and then transfer to another train bound for Ishizuchiyama Station. A leisurely 20 minute walk from there will lead you to the shrine distinguished by its torii gate.<ref name=":0" />
Surrounded by bamboo groves and moss covered {{ill|Cypress|en|Cypress|qid=Q14169641|lt=Cypress|1=Cypress}} trees Ishizuchi jinja Shrine exudes tranquility. Within the shrine grounds stand statues depicting the {{ill|Tengu|en|Tengu|qid=Q742360|lt=Tengu|1=Tengu}} mountain spirits. Ascending a flight of stairs within the shrine rewards visitors with views of the [[Seto Inland Sea]].<ref name=":0" />
== Facilities ==
=== Maegamiji Temple === [[File:Maegamizi01.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Maegami-ji|ja|前神寺|de|lt=Maegami-ji temple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maegami-ji|1=Maegami-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11397792}}]] Adjacent to Ishizuchi jinja Shrine lies {{ill|Maegami-ji|ja|前神寺|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maegami-ji|1=Maegami-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11397792}} Temple as it once served as the primary sanctuary, for devotees of Mt. Ishizuchi.<ref name=":0" /> It was built during the rein of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|qid=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Ishizuchisan Konjikiin Maegamiji – Shikoku 88 Temple Pilgrimage|url=https://88shikokuhenro.jp/en/ishizuchisan-konjikiin-maegamiji/|access-date=2024-03-29|language=en}}</ref> It is temple 64 of the 88 shrine pilgrimage.<ref name=":1" /> {{clear}}
=== Chugu Jojusha Shrine === [[File:Ishizuchi Shrine Mountaintop Honden.jpg|thumb|Chugu Jojusha Shrine on the summit of Mount Ishizuchi]] Within the Ishizuchi jinja Shrine compound lies the Chugu Jojusha Shrine, positioned at the beginning of the Mt. Ishizuchi Omotesando trail. This shrine marks the starting point for hikers embarking on their journey, up the mountain. Upon reaching the summit travelers will encounter another shrine affiliated with the Ishizuchi complex providing views of the surrounding mountains and [[Seto Inland Sea]].<ref name=":0" />
==Gallery== ===Ishizuchi Shrine=== <gallery> File:Ishizuchi Jinja Shinmon.jpg|alt=Ishizuchi Shrine's Shinmon, a traditional gate|Ishizuchi Shrine's Shinmon File:Ishizuchi Tozan Ropeway, Ehime (17302809620).jpg|alt=Ropeway to Ishizuchi Mountain in Ehime|Ishizuchi Tozan Ropeway in Ehime File:Ishizuchi2.jpg|Ishizuchi Shrine on the mountaintop File:Isiduti 31.jpg|alt=Thirty-first photograph in a series on Ishizuchi|Ishizuchi Shrine's Shinmon File:Ishizuchi Shrine Mountaintop Honden.jpg|alt=Honden at the mountaintop of Ishizuchi Shrine|Mountaintop Honden of Ishizuchi Shrine File:Ishizuchi Shrine Mountaintop Honden 2.jpg|Ishizuchi Shrine Mountaintop Honden File:Ishizuchi Shrine Honden porch.jpg|alt=The porch of Ishizuchi Shrine's Honden captured in September 2019|Honden Porch, Ishizuchi Shrine, September 2019 File:Ishizuchi Shrine and Mountain marker.jpg|alt=Marker at Ishizuchi Shrine with the mountain in the background|Ishizuchi Shrine and Mountain Marker File:Isidutisaniwakura01.jpg|alt=First view of Iwakura at Ishizuchi|Iwakura at Ishizuchi, First View File:Ishizuchi Sendatsu.jpg|alt=Sendatsu, or spiritual leader, of Ishizuchi|Sendatsu of Ishizuchi File:Isidutittyouzyousya.jpg|Teahouse at Ishizuchi Shrine File:Izuchi Shrine Statues.jpg|alt=Eleventh view within Ishizuchi Shrine series|Ishizuchi Shrine, Statues File:Isidutizinzya wagesa.jpg|alt=Wagesa, a priestly garment, at Ishizuchi Shrine|Wagesa at Ishizuchi Shrine File:Ishizuchi jinja yōhaiden 2014.jpg|alt=First view within Ishizuchi Shrine series|Ishizuchi Shrine, View 1 File:Isidutizinzya07.jpg|alt=Seventh view within Ishizuchi Shrine series|Ishizuchi Shrine, View 7 File:Ishizuchi Shrine Mountaintop Honden 3.jpg|alt=Third view of the mountaintop Honden at Ishizuchi Shrine|Mountaintop Honden of Ishizuchi Shrine, View 3 File:Ishizuchi Shrine Statue.jpg|Statue at Ishizuchi Shrine File:Isidutizinzya5.jpg|alt=Fifth view within Ishizuchi Shrine series|Ishizuchi Shrine, Soreiden File:Ishizuchi Jinja Gohei.jpg|alt=Gohei, sacred Shinto symbols, at Ishizuchi Shrine|Gohei at Ishizuchi Shrine File:Maezyagamori.jpg|View of Maezyagamori File:Ishizuchi jinja yōhaiden.jpg|alt=Remote worship hall at Ishizuchi Shrine, July 2024|Remote Worship Hall, Ishizuchi Shrine, July 2024 File:石鎚神社本殿.JPG|alt=Main hall of Ishizuchi Shrine|Main Hall of Ishizuchi Shrine File:石鎚神社祖霊殿.JPG|Ancestral Spirit Hall at Ishizuchi Shrine File:石鎚神社神門.JPG|alt=Shinmon gate at Ishizuchi Shrine|Shinmon Gate at Ishizuchi Shrine File:石鎚神社花表.JPG|alt=Floral display at Ishizuchi Shrine|Floral Display at Ishizuchi Shrine </gallery>

File:Maegamiji 05.JPG|alt=Fifth photo capturing Maegamiji Temple|Maegamiji Temple: Exterior View 05 File:Maegamiji 06.JPG|alt=Sixth photo capturing Maegamiji Temple|Maegamiji Temple: Exterior View 06 File:Maegamiji 07.JPG|alt=Seventh and largest photo of Maegamiji Temple|Maegamiji Temple: Exterior View 07 File:Maegamiji 20201215 1.jpg|alt=First photo from December 15, 2020, showing Maegamiji 31, 2020 File:Maegamiji 20220918 1.jpg|alt=Photo from September 18, 2022, featuring Maegamiji Temple|Maegamiji Temple: September 18, 2022 File:Maegamiji badge.jpg|alt=Badge featuring Maegamiji Temple symbol|Maegamiji Temple Badge File:Maegamiji gongendou 01.jpg|alt=Photo of the Gongendou at Maegamiji Temple|Maegamiji Temple: Gongendou File:Maegamiji yakusidou gomadou 01.jpg|alt=Photo of the Yakusidou Gomadou at Maegamiji Temple|Maegamiji Temple: Yakusidou Gomadou File:Maegamiji01.jpg|alt=Photo of the serene ambiance at Maegamiji Temple|Maegamiji Temple: Serene Atmosphere File:Maegamiji20210202 1.jpg|alt=Photo from February 2, 2021, featuring Maegamiji Temple|Maegamiji Temple: February 2, 2021 File:Maegamiji20210206 1.jpg|alt=Photo from February 6, 2021, depicting Maegamiji Temple|Maegamiji Temple: February 6, 2021 File:Maegami-ji in Main Hall.jpg|alt=Photo of the Main Hall at Maegamiji Temple|Maegamiji Temple: Main Hall File:Maegami-ji sign.jpg|alt=Alternate view of the sign at Maegamiji Temple|Maegamiji Temple: Sign (Alternate View 1) File:Maegami-ji sign 2.jpg|alt=Another alternate view of the sign at Maegamiji Temple|Maegamiji Temple: Sign (Alternate View 2) File:Maegamizi 02.jpg|alt=Second photo featuring Maegamiji Temple|Maegamiji Temple: Exterior View 02 File:Fire ritual at Maegami-ji.jpg|alt=Fire ritual ceremony at Maegamiji Temple|Maegamiji Temple: Fire Ritual Ceremony File:Maegamizi 11.jpg|alt=Eleventh photo capturing Maegamiji Temple|Maegamiji Temple: Exterior View 11 File:Maegamizi01.jpg|alt=Photo showcasing the grandeur of Maegamiji Temple|Maegamiji Temple: Grandeur File:Maegamizi02.jpg|alt=Second photo showcasing Maegamiji Temple|Sakura at the temple File:Maegamizi04.jpg|alt=Fourth photo showcasing Maegamiji Temple|Maegamiji Temple: Exterior View 04 File:Open air fire ritual at Maegami-ji.jpg|alt=Open-air fire ritual at Maegamiji Temple|Maegamiji Temple: Open-air Fire Ritual File:Fudo Myoo statue at Maegami-ji closeup.jpg|alt=Close-up of Fudo Myoo statue at Maegamiji Temple|Maegamiji Temple: Fudo Myoo Statue (Close-up) File:Procession at Maegami-ji.jpg|alt=Procession taking place at Maegamiji Temple|Maegamiji Temple: Procession </gallery>
